title: main.c: standalone LoRa RX handler does not consume packet data
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/src/main.c:69-76

## Issue
The lora_rx_handler() in standalone mode logs received packets but does not process them:

```c
static void lora_rx_handler(const uint8_t *data, size_t len,
                            int16_t rssi, int8_t snr, void *user_data)
{
    ARG_UNUSED(user_data);

    LOG_INF("RX: %zu bytes (standalone mode - not processed)", len);
    LOG_INF("  RSSI=%d dBm, SNR=%d dB", rssi, snr);
}
```

The (standalone mode - not processed) message explains this is intentional, but:
1. No diagnostic hex dump of received data for debugging
2. No counter of packets received for health monitoring
3. A tester may not realize packets are being silently dropped

## What goes wrong
- Debugging interop issues is harder without seeing packet contents
- No way to verify frames are being received correctly without adding debug code
- Pattern may be copied to production code without the not processed comment

## Fix
Consider adding:
1. LOG_HEXDUMP_DBG(data, MIN(len, 32), "RX payload") for debug builds
2. A static counter logged periodically (e.g., every 10 packets)
